title: podman-remote: logs: missing newlines
body:
both root and rootless, on current master. Example uses root because rootless-run doesn't work (#6538):
```console
# MYSOCK=unix:/tmp/mypodmansock 
# ./bin/podman system service --timeout=0 unix:/tmp/mypodmansock &
[1] 465801

# ./bin/podman-remote --url $MYSOCK run --name foo alpine sh -c "echo hi;echo bye"
hi
bye

# ./bin/podman-remote --url $MYSOCK logs foo
hibye#   <--- the hash indicates my shell prompt
```

comments.body:
If you do `bin/podman logs foo` does it do the right thing?
Nice catch.

Oops! Sorry, meant to include that in the first place. Yes, `bin/podman` (not remote) works:
```console
$ ./bin/podman logs foo
hi
bye
```
...and, just to confirm that the problem is with `podman-remote logs`, running the initial container with podman (not remote) and pulling logs with podman-remote also has missing newlines:
```console
$ ./bin/podman run --name foo alpine sh -c "echo hi from podman;echo bye from podman"
hi from podman
bye from podman
$ ./bin/podman-remote --url $MYSOCK logs foo
hi from podmanbye from podman%     <--- missing newline
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing as it has been fixed in the main branch.
